jd_text: Job Summary: Responsible for overseeing the hospital’s information technology (IT) and digital transformation initiatives. The Chief Information Officer (CIO) will develop and implement an IT strategy that aligns with the hospital’s mission to deliver high-quality patient care while ensuring operational efficiency through innovative technological solutions. Design, implement, and manage all hospital information systems, including electronic health records (EHR), telehealth platforms, and cybersecurity measures. The CIO will work collaboratively with clinical and administrative leaders to assess technology needs, enhance data security, and ensure compliance with healthcare regulations. Key Responsibilities: Develop and execute the hospital’s IT strategy to align with business objectives and enhance patient care delivery. Oversee the implementation and management of hospital information systems, including EHR, telemedicine, and cybersecurity. Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations, data security, and patient confidentiality standards. Lead a team of IT professionals and foster a culture of innovation and continuous improvement. Collaborate with clinical and operational leaders to identify technology needs and translate them into effective IT solutions. Stay current with emerging technologies and industry best practices to drive improvements in patient care and operational efficiency. Qualifications: Master’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field; MBA is a plus. Minimum 10 years of progressive experience in IT leadership roles, preferably in a healthcare setting. Proven experience in managing large-scale IT projects, system implementations, and vendor relationships. Strong knowledge of healthcare regulations, technology trends, and best practices in health IT. Excellent communication and leadership skills, with the ability to build relationships across diverse teams.
